response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger allergic reactions in some individuals  
allergic reactions can cause severe symptoms such as difficulty breathing and swelling of the lips  
these reactions can be life-threatening and require immediate medical attention  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot eat them and must avoid them in all forms  
the question is about why they can't live underwater  
the reason is related to the physical properties of peanuts and their inability to withstand underwater conditions  

Sec. 2  
peanuts are not designed to survive in water and their structure makes them unsuitable for underwater environments  
they are made of a hard outer shell and a soft interior that would be affected by water pressure  
the outer shell would crack and the interior would be damaged  
this makes it impossible for them to function properly in water  
the question is about why they can't live underwater
